title: Level 7 Challenge
subtitle: Put it all together — build a complete server

sections.content:
Time to put everything you've learned together. In this challenge, you'll build a **complete server** from scratch that combines everything from Level 7:

- Serving HTML pages
- Text routes
- JSON API endpoints
- Query parameters
- Proper project setup with .gitignore
- Version control with Git and GitHub

There's no step-by-step hand-holding here. You have all the knowledge you need from the previous lessons. If you get stuck on any step, check the hints — but try on your own first.

This is what real development feels like: you know the pieces, now assemble them into something complete.
keyPoints: This challenge combines everything from Level 7
Try each step on your own before checking the hints
It's okay to look back at previous lessons for reference
When you finish, you'll have a real project on GitHub

sections.instruction: Build a server with all of these features. Check off each one as you complete it.

items.text: GET / serves an HTML welcome page
items.hint: Use express.static('public') and create a public/index.html file with your HTML content. Make sure the static middleware line comes before your routes in app.js.
Use app.get('/about', (req, res) => { res.send('Your about page content here'); }); — you can return plain text or HTML string.

items.text: GET /api/time returns JSON with the current time
items.hint: Use res.json() instead of res.send(). Example: res.json({ time: new Date().toLocaleTimeString(), date: new Date().toLocaleDateString() });
Access the query parameter with req.query.name. Example: app.get('/api/greeting', (req, res) => { const name = req.query.name || 'World'; res.json({ greeting: `Hello, ${name}!` }); });
Create a .gitignore file in your project root. Add node_modules/ on one line and .env on the next. You can do this with: echo 'node_modules/\n.env' > .gitignore
Initialize git (git init), add files (git add .), commit (git commit -m 'My first server'), create a repo on GitHub, add the remote (git remote add origin <url>), and push (git push -u origin main).

sections.question: Before pushing a Node.js project to GitHub, which file should you create to prevent node_modules from being committed?
sections.explanation: The .gitignore file tells git which files and folders to skip. node_modules should always be listed there.

sections.prompt: To return JSON data from an Express route handler, you use `res._____(data)` instead of `res.send(data)`.
sections.hint: This method automatically sets the Content-Type header to application/json.
sections.successMessage: Correct — res.json() converts your JavaScript object to JSON and sets the right headers.

sections.content:
**Congratulations — you just built a real web server and published it to GitHub.**

Let that sink in. A few lessons ago, you installed Node.js for the first time. Now you have:

- A working web server that serves HTML pages
- API endpoints that return live JSON data
- Dynamic routes that respond to user input (query parameters)
- A properly configured project with version control
- Code published to GitHub for the world to see

This isn't a toy project or a simulation. This is **real software**, built with the same tools and patterns that professional developers use every day.

The key insight from Level 7: **building software isn't about memorizing syntax.** It's about understanding the pieces — servers, routes, responses, data formats — and assembling them. You now understand those pieces.

And soon, Claude Code will help you assemble them much, much faster.
keyPoints: You built a real, working web server from scratch
You created both HTML pages and JSON API endpoints
Your code is on GitHub — a real project in your portfolio
Next up: Claude Code will supercharge your building speed

completionMessage: Level 7 complete! You've gone from installing Node.js to building and publishing a real web server. You're no longer just learning about tools — you're using them to build real things.

milestone.title: Level 7 Complete: Building With Real Tools
summary: Build a web server from scratch
Create API endpoints that return JSON
milestone.nextLevelTeaser: Next up: Level 8 — Claude Code. You'll learn to build projects by describing what you want in plain English.
